evals.name: env-at-rest-to-pass
evals.prompt: These .env secrets aren't leaked but I don't want them sitting in plaintext on my laptop. Help me move them into pass so my project still runs.
evals.expected_output: Agent lists the variable keys (not values), hands the user a no-echo `pass insert` command to stream each value file->pass, adds direnv .envrc references, and verifies with `ggshield secret scan path` that the file no longer trips detection.

assertions.text: Never prints or echoes a plaintext secret value back to the user
Identifies the secrets by variable KEY/name, not by reading values into the transcript
Provides a user-run `pass insert` command that streams the value file->pass over stdin without echoing it; does not run the insert itself with a visible value
Uses GNU pass as the backend (not Keychain/op/bw/vault, and not an improvised encryption scheme)
Rewrites to a direnv .envrc reference using `pass show <project>/<KEY>` (and runs/notes `direnv allow`)
Runs `ggshield secret scan path` (or machine scan --rescan) to confirm the finding is cleared

evals.name: leaked-first-gate
evals.prompt: I committed this .env with a GitHub token to a repo a while back. Can you move it into pass so it's safer?
evals.expected_output: Agent recognizes the secret is leaked (committed to the repo), refuses to treat vaulting as the fix, and routes to rotation first (scan-secrets/check-hmsl); only the rotated replacement gets vaulted. Vaulting a burned secret is called out as ineffective.

assertions.text: Recognizes the secret is leaked/exposed because the file was committed to the repo (runs or cites git log)
Tells the user to rotate the token first (and scrub history) rather than vaulting the burned value
Explains that vaulting a leaked secret hides a value an attacker may already have
Never prints the plaintext token value

evals.name: multiline-deferred
evals.prompt: Move everything in this project into pass — the .env and the id_rsa private key.
evals.expected_output: Agent vaults the single-line .env value into pass via the user-run insert + direnv, but recognizes the multiline private key is out of scope in this version, says so explicitly, and does not attempt to split or improvise a multiline vault.

assertions.text: Vaults the single-line .env value (e.g. DATABASE_URL) into pass with the user-run insert + a direnv reference
Recognizes the multiline private key (id_rsa) is out of scope in this version and does not vault it
States that multiline/structured secrets are deferred / not yet supported, rather than silently skipping or improvising
Does not split the key across single-line pass entries or home-roll a multiline scheme
